settings.title: Why yelling and shock collars fail
settings.text: Yelling adds to the chaos, and shock collars create fear. Neither breaks the actual barking loop.

settings.title: The flat-tone device problem
settings.text: Dogs quickly get used to single-frequency devices and start ignoring them.

settings.title: Why traditional methods keep failing to stop the noise.
settings.text: When a dog is locked into a barking spiral, they aren't listening. Yelling just sounds like you're barking with them. Shock collars create anxiety that can make reactivity worse. And cheap flat-tone devices lose their effectiveness in days.
settings.analogy_title: You need to break the pattern, not punish the dog.
settings.analogy_text: ByeBark uses a shifting frequency that instantly captures their attention, pulling them out of the barking loop so you can redirect their behavior.

settings.question: Is ByeBark a shock device?
settings.answer: No. ByeBark is positioned as an ultrasonic bark-control tool, not a shock collar or prong-based routine.

settings.question: What kind of barking is it designed for?
settings.answer: It is best framed around common household triggers like fence fighting, delivery-driver barking, and overstimulated barking at home.

settings.question: Will it work for every dog?
settings.answer: Results can vary by dog, environment, and training consistency. The safer promise is that it is designed to help interrupt unwanted barking quickly for many households.

settings.question: Where should I keep it?
settings.answer: Most homes will keep it near the front door, yard access, or other places where barking tends to start first so it is easy to reach in the moment.

settings.question: Is the sound painful for my dog?
settings.answer: No, the ultrasonic sound is 100% pain-free. It is designed to be surprising and attention-grabbing, not painful.

settings.question: What is Frequency-Shift Technology?
settings.answer: Unlike cheap devices that use a single flat tone, ByeBark shifts its frequency between 30-40kHz. This prevents your dog from getting used to the sound and ignoring it over time.

settings.question: Is it waterproof?
settings.answer: ByeBark is water-resistant and suitable for outdoor use in light rain, but should not be submerged in water.

settings.question: How long does it take to see results?
settings.answer: Many owners see an immediate interruption in barking. With consistent use alongside our free training protocol, most dogs break the habit for good in just 3 days.
